title: SimResult
description: The persisted, replayable record of one simulation run produced by the polis/sim/ substrate kernel. Closes the loop on sim_config.schema.json's output_schema_ref. SimResult is the orchestration-side envelope wrapping the kernel's in-process FinalState (polis/sim/contract.py) with the metadata required for replay, audit, ledger settlement, and narrative surfaces (V0-PLAN §3.8). Per DECISION-SIM-ENGINE.md. v0.1.0 ships the universal envelope + Event sub-schema; per-industry outcome shapes are declared by each industry's SimConfig.output_schema_ref override. v0.1.1 adds world_time_at_start and world_time_at_end per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md (kernel emits events at world-time pace; wall-clock and world-clock are tracked separately).

sim_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this sim run. Convention: 'sim_' + ULID or UUID. Used as the foreign key from ledger settlement entries, narrative-surface references, and replay-recording lookups.

industry_id.description: Foreign key to the industry whose SimConfig drove this run (e.g. 'football-modern-earth', 'basketball-toy'). Mirrors SimConfig.industry_id.

world_id.description: World this sim ran in. Required for V0-PLAN §5.1 rule 5 (world_id on every queryable row). Omitted only for global / cross-world sims, which V0 does not have.

kernel_version.description: Semver of polis/sim/ at run time. Recorded so replays know which kernel to use; mismatched kernels can produce divergent event logs even with the same seed.

config_version.description: Semver of the SimConfig at run time (SimConfig.metadata.version). Rule changes that affect output distributions bump this; replays against newer configs must reconcile or fail.

seed.description: RNG seed passed to SeededRandom. Load-bearing for determinism — calibration suites depend on bit-identical event_log given the same (kernel_version, config_version, seed, stub_llm_hook).

started_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when sim execution began. ISO 8601.

ended_at.description: Wall-clock UTC timestamp when sim execution finished. ISO 8601.

world_time_at_start.description: Per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md: world-clock timestamp when the sim began emitting events. Under the one-clock-no-fast-forward model, the kernel emits events at world-time pace — this is the world clock value at the first event. sim_duration_seconds equals (world_time_at_end - world_time_at_start).

world_time_at_end.description: Per DECISION-TIME-MODEL.md: world-clock timestamp when the sim finished emitting events. (world_time_at_end - world_time_at_start) should equal sim_duration_seconds. Note: ended_at (wall-clock) may differ — at 12× world rate, a 90-world-minute match takes 7.5 wall-min for emission.

sim_duration_seconds.description: How far sim_time advanced during the run. Driver-defined unit — for football this is 5400 (90 min) + injury time; for a steel shift 28800 (8 hours).

wall_duration_ms.description: How long the run took in real wall-clock milliseconds. For calibration runs, this measures kernel performance; for live runs, this is dominated by LLM-hook latency.

terminated_by.description:
How the sim ended.
- 'duration', 'target_state', 'event_count': matched the corresponding EndCondition in SimConfig.
- 'queue_drained': no further events scheduled and no end-condition met (legitimate for some sims, suspicious for others).
- 'explicit_end': a handler called sim.end().
- 'error': uncaught exception (see error field).
- 'external': workflow cancelled the run (e.g., player abandoned).

termination_detail.description: Optional extra context for terminated_by. e.g., for 'error', { error_type, message, traceback }; for 'external', { cancelled_by, reason }.

event_log.description: Full play-by-play in sim_time order. Each Event mirrors the polis.sim.events.Event dataclass. Same schema serves every industry — payload carries the domain-specific detail.

outcome.description: Industry-specific summary of how the sim ended. Open object — the kernel does not introspect. Per-industry SimConfig.output_schema_ref points at the schema this outcome conforms to (e.g., football: { score: {home, away}, winner, top_scorer, key_events }; court: { verdict, damages, costs_award }; election: { winner, margin, turnout, swing_constituencies }).

llm_hook.description: Which LLM hook drove decision points, and any replay metadata. Determines whether the run is bit-replayable.

deterministic.description: True for stub/random/recorded hooks; false for live LLM-routed hooks. Calibration suites must use deterministic hooks.

model_version.description: If applicable — pinned model identifier (e.g. 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20251022'). Required when deterministic=false for replay forensics per V0-PLAN §3.3.

recording_ref.description: Foreign key to a recorded-LLM-responses payload (V0.5+ recording-hook feature). Enables exact replay of a live run.

replay_hash.description: Optional hash of (kernel_version, config_version, seed, llm_hook.name, event_log) — fast equality check for 'did two runs produce the same outcome'. Used by calibration drift detection.

metadata.description: Free-form: workflow_id that triggered the sim, parent_sim_id for nested sims, calibration_run flag, notes.

workflow_id.description: Temporal workflow ID that triggered this sim — for trace correlation.

calibration_run.description: True when this run is part of a Monte Carlo calibration suite. Outcome is compared against per-industry target distributions; not persisted to live narrative surfaces.

parent_sim_id.description: For nested sims (e.g., a court-case sim spawned during a hostile-takeover sim) — back-reference to the outer sim_id.

Event.description: One thing that happened in the sim, at a moment in sim_time. Mirrors polis.sim.events.Event dataclass. Industry-agnostic envelope; payload carries domain detail.

sim_time.description: Seconds since sim start. Driver-defined mapping from native time unit.

actor_id.description: Which actor caused this event. Null for environmental events (kickoff, weather, regulator-driven, automatic timer).

payload.description: Industry-specific detail. The kernel does not introspect. Example football payloads: {target: [28, -3], xg: 0.34}; example court payloads: {exhibit_id: 'E-7', sustained: false}.

sequence.description: Monotonic counter set by the kernel on emit. Tie-breaker for same-sim_time events; guarantees deterministic ordering across runs with the same seed.
